# Estimation <span class="badge badge-draft1">✎ Very rough draft</span>

```{r}
#| include: false
# if it is available, run the setup script that tells quarto to round all df/tibble outputs to three decimal places
if(file.exists("../_setup.R")){source("../_setup.R")}
```

```{r}
# dependencies
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(furrr) 
library(parameters)
library(stringr)
library(forcats)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)
library(ggstance)
library(patchwork)
library(janitor)
library(effectsize)

# set up parallelization
plan(multisession)
```

## Simulation

```{r}

# functions for simulation
generate_data <- function(n_per_condition,
                          mean_control,
                          mean_intervention,
                          sd) {
  
  data_control <- 
    tibble(condition = "control",
           score = rnorm(n = n_per_condition, mean = mean_control, sd = sd))
  
  data_intervention <- 
    tibble(condition = "intervention",
           score = rnorm(n = n_per_condition, mean = mean_intervention, sd = sd))
  
  data_combined <- 
    bind_rows(data_control,
              data_intervention) |>
    mutate(condition = factor(condition, level = c("intervention", "control")))
  
  return(data_combined)
}

analyse <- function(data){
  fit <- t.test(
    formula = score ~ condition,
    var.equal = TRUE, # students t test: assumes equal variances
    data = data
  )
  
  results <- fit %>%
    model_parameters() %>%
    as_tibble() %>% 
    janitor::clean_names() %>% 
    # select-and-rename the columns of interest
    select(t,
           df = df_error,
           p,
           mean_diff = difference,
           mean_diff_ci_lower = ci_low,
           mean_diff_ci_upper = ci_high)
  
  return(results)
}

# simulation parameters
experiment_parameters <- expand_grid(
  n_per_condition = seq(from = 10, to = 100, by = 10),
  mean_control = 4,
  mean_intervention = 5,
  sd = 1.5,
  iteration = 1:1000
) 

# set seed
set.seed(43)

# run simulation
simulation <- experiment_parameters |>
  mutate(generated_data = future_pmap(.l = list(n_per_condition = n_per_condition, 
                                                mean_control = mean_control,
                                                mean_intervention = mean_intervention,
                                                sd = sd),
                                      .f = generate_data,
                                      .progress = TRUE,
                                      .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE))) |>
  mutate(results = future_pmap(.l = list(data = generated_data),
                               .f = analyse,
                               .progress = TRUE,
                               .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE)))

```

## Estimated mean difference by sample size

```{r fig.height=5, fig.width=6}

# wrangle
simulation |>
  unnest(results) |>
  # plot
  ggplot(aes(n_per_condition*2, mean_diff, color = mean_diff > 0)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "purple") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = breaks_pretty(n = 10),
                     name = "Total N") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = breaks_pretty(n = 10),
                     #limits = c(0,1),
                     name = "Sample mean difference") +
  theme_linedraw() +
  scale_color_viridis_d(begin = 0.4, end = 0.6) +
  ggtitle("Population Mean difference = 1") 

```

```{r fig.height=8, fig.width=6}

# wrangle
simulation |>
  unnest(results) |>
  group_by(n_per_condition) |>
  slice(1:10) |>
  ungroup() |>
  # plot
  ggplot(aes(mean_diff, iteration, color = mean_diff > 0)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "purple") +
  geom_point() +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = "reverse",
                     breaks = breaks_pretty(n = 10),
                     name = "Study (iteration)") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = breaks_pretty(n = 3),
                     #limits = c(0,1),
                     name = "Sample mean difference") +
  theme_linedraw() +
  theme(panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank()) +
  ggtitle("Population Mean difference = 1") +
  facet_wrap(~ n_per_condition, ncol = 2)

```



```{r fig.height=8, fig.width=6}

# wrangle
simulation |>
  unnest(results) |>
  group_by(n_per_condition) |>
  slice(1:10) |>
  ungroup() |>
  # plot
  ggplot(aes(mean_diff, iteration, color = mean_diff_ci_lower > 0)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "purple") +
  geom_linerangeh(aes(xmin = mean_diff_ci_lower, xmax = mean_diff_ci_upper)) +
  geom_point() +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = "reverse",
                     breaks = breaks_pretty(n = 10),
                     name = "Study (iteration)") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = breaks_pretty(n = 3),
                     #limits = c(0,1),
                     name = "Sample mean difference") +
  theme_linedraw() +
  theme(panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank()) +
  ggtitle("Population Mean difference = 1") +
  facet_wrap(~ n_per_condition, ncol = 2)

```


```{r}

simulation |>
  unnest(results) |>
  # plot
  ggplot(aes(n_per_condition*2, mean_diff_ci_upper - mean_diff_ci_lower)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = breaks_pretty(n = 10),
                     name = "Total N") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = breaks_pretty(n = 10),
                     #limits = c(0,1),
                     name = "CI width") +
  theme_linedraw() +
  scale_color_viridis_d(begin = 0.3, end = 0.7) +
  ggtitle("Population Mean difference = 1")

summary_ci_width <- simulation |>
  unnest(results) |>
  mutate(ci_width = mean_diff_ci_upper - mean_diff_ci_lower) |>
  group_by(n_per_condition) |>
  summarize(mean_ci_width = mean(ci_width),
            min_ci_width = min(ci_width),
            max_ci_width = max(ci_width))

library(ggstance)

ggplot(summary_ci_width, aes(n_per_condition*2, mean_ci_width)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  #geom_linerange(aes(ymin = min_ci_width, ymax = max_ci_width)) +
  geom_point() +
  #scale_y_continuous(name = "95% CI width\n(mean [min, max])", limits = c(0, NA)) +
  scale_y_continuous(name = "Mean 95% CI width", limits = c(0, NA)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = breaks_pretty(n = 9), name = "Total N") +
  theme_linedraw() +
  scale_color_viridis_d(begin = 0.3, end = 0.7) +
  ggtitle("Population Mean difference = 1") 

```

-   Each point is a difference-in-means from one dataset (i.e., one iteration).
-   The population difference-in-means is 0.5. Notice how the distribution of sample difference-in-means values vary by sample size: difference-in-means values above 2.0 and below -0.5 are observed when sample size is very small. As sample size gets larger, they stabilize. However, individual datasets still generate difference-in-means values that diverge substantially from the true value of 0.5.

### Ordered by statistical significance

Let's color the difference-in-meanss by their statistical significance.

```{r fig.height=5, fig.width=6}
simulation |>
  unnest(results) |>
  mutate(significant = p < .05) |>
  # plot
  ggplot(aes(n_per_condition*2, mean_diff, color = significant)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = breaks_pretty(n = 10),
                     name = "Total N") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = breaks_pretty(n = 10),
                     #limits = c(0,1),
                     name = "difference-in-means") +
  theme_linedraw() +
  scale_color_viridis_d(begin = 0.3, end = 0.7, direction = -1) +
  ggtitle("Population difference-in-means = 0.5")
```

-   There is clearly a cut-off value for each sample size that determines when a difference-in-means value is associated with a significant vs. non-significant t-test p-value. What determines this cut-off? Do you have an intuition for it?

### Average effect size for significant effects

Quick aside: Significant results are more likely to be published than non significant ones. If we only look at the significant results, what is the average effect size by N?

Plot only the significant effect sizes, and add their means.

```{r fig.height=5, fig.width=6}
simulation |>
  unnest(results) |>
  mutate(significant = p < .05) |>
  filter(significant) |>
  # plot
  ggplot(aes(n_per_condition*2, mean_diff, color = significant)) +
  geom_jitter(alpha = 0.25) +
  # add mean points 
  stat_summary(fun = "mean",
               geom = "point",
               size = 3,
               color = "#35608DFF") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = breaks_pretty(n = 10),
                     name = "Total N") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = breaks_pretty(n = 10),
                     #limits = c(0,1),
                     name = "difference-in-means") +
  theme_linedraw() +
  scale_color_viridis_d(begin = 0.3, end = 0.7, direction = -1) +
  ggtitle("Population difference-in-means = 0.5")
```

-   Back to the q: There is clearly a cut-off value for each sample size that determines when a difference-in-means value is associated with a significant vs. non-significant t-test p-value. What determines this cut-off? Do you have an intuition for it?

## difference-in-means and its 95% CIs

To understand it further, let's add the 95% Confidence Intervals.

### Randomly select one dataset per sample size

To understand the plot, we'll plot just one data set (iteration) for each sample size.

Re-run the chunk to select new random values. Notice how the magnitude of the difference-in-means values 'dance' around between data sets due to random sampling error, but the width of the Confidence Intervals do not. Confidence width is steady as it is a function of sample size.

```{r}
simulation |>
  unnest(results) |>
  mutate(significant = p < .05) |>
  # randomly sample 1 effects size per sample size
  group_by(n_per_condition) |>
  slice_sample(n = 1) |>
  ungroup() |>
  # plot
  ggplot(aes(n_per_condition*2, mean_diff, color = significant)) +
  geom_point() +
  # 95% CIs
  geom_linerange(aes(ymin = mean_diff_ci_lower, ymax = mean_diff_ci_upper)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = breaks_pretty(n = 10),
                     name = "Total N") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = breaks_pretty(n = 10),
                     #limits = c(0,1),
                     name = "difference-in-means") +
  theme_linedraw() +
  scale_color_viridis_d(begin = 0.3, end = 0.7) +
  ggtitle("Population difference-in-means = 0.5\nOne randomly selected dataset per sample size")
```

-   Significant when 95% CI excludes zero effect size

### All datasets

Now let's plot all the data sets (iterations).

```{r fig.height=10, fig.width=12}
simulation |>
  unnest(results) |>
  mutate(significant = p < .05,
         total_n = paste("N =", n_per_condition*2),
         total_n = fct_reorder(total_n, as.numeric(str_extract(total_n, "\\d+")))) |>
  # plot
  ggplot(aes(iteration, mean_diff, color = significant)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_linerange(aes(ymin = mean_diff_ci_lower, ymax = mean_diff_ci_upper), alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = breaks_pretty(n = 10),
                     name = "Iteration") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = breaks_pretty(n = 10),
                     #limits = c(0,1),
                     name = "difference-in-means") +
  theme_linedraw() +
  scale_color_viridis_d(begin = 0.3, end = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ total_n, ncol = 3, scales = "free_y")
```

### Ordered by difference-in-means

The above plot is hard to understand. Let's order the difference-in-meanss from smallest to largest, so the small ones are on the left and the large ones are on the right.

```{r fig.height=10, fig.width=12}
simulation |>
  unnest(results) |>
  mutate(significant = p < .05,
         total_n = paste("N =", n_per_condition*2),
         total_n = fct_reorder(total_n, as.numeric(str_extract(total_n, "\\d+")))) |>
  arrange(n_per_condition, mean_diff) |>
  group_by(n_per_condition) |>
  mutate(rank = row_number()) |>
  ungroup() |>
  # plot
  ggplot(aes(rank, mean_diff, color = significant)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_linerange(aes(ymin = mean_diff_ci_lower, ymax = mean_diff_ci_upper), alpha = 0.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = breaks_pretty(n = 10),
                     name = "Ranked iteration") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = breaks_pretty(n = 10),
                     #limits = c(0,1),
                     name = "difference-in-means") +
  theme_linedraw() +
  scale_color_viridis_d(begin = 0.3, end = 0.7) +
  facet_wrap(~ total_n, ncol = 3)
```

-   Notice the relationship between 95% CI and p value significance.
-   A certain percentage of difference-in-meanss are green vs. blue. What is this percentage also called? What statistical property?

## Mean difference-in-means and mean interval width by sample size

Now let's average over the difference-in-meanss in each condition to find the mean difference-in-means and its mean 95% CIs.

```{r fig.height=2.5, fig.width=6}
simulation_summary <- simulation |>
  # unnest results
  unnest(results) |>
  group_by(n_per_condition) |>
  summarize(proportion_significant = mean(p < .05), 
            mean_mean_diff = mean(mean_diff),
            mean_mean_diff_ci_lower = mean(mean_diff_ci_lower),
            mean_mean_diff_ci_upper = mean(mean_diff_ci_upper)) |>
  mutate(centered_mean_mean_diff_ci_lower = mean_mean_diff_ci_lower - mean_mean_diff,
         centered_mean_mean_diff_ci_upper = mean_mean_diff_ci_upper - mean_mean_diff)

# plot results
p1 <- ggplot(simulation_summary, aes(n_per_condition*2, mean_mean_diff)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_linerange(aes(ymin = mean_mean_diff_ci_lower, ymax = mean_mean_diff_ci_upper)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = breaks_pretty(n = 10),
                     name = "Total sample size") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = breaks_pretty(n = 5),
                     #limits = c(0,1),
                     name = "Mean difference-in-means\n(and mean 95% CIs)") +
  theme_linedraw() +
  ggtitle("Population difference-in-means = 0.5")

p1
```

## Mean difference-in-means and power by sample size

```{r fig.height=5, fig.width=6}
p2 <- ggplot(simulation_summary, aes(n_per_condition*2, proportion_significant)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.05, linetype = "dotted") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.80, linetype = "dotted") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = breaks_pretty(n = 10),
                     name = "Total sample size") +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = breaks_pretty(n = 5),
                     limits = c(0,1),
                     name = "Proportion of significant\np-values") +
  theme_linedraw() 

p1 + p2 + plot_layout(ncol = 1)
```

## Exercise: Power for equivalence test

See Lakens et al., 2018 for a tutorial on the concepts below.

Using a Smallest Effect Size of Interest (SESOI) of difference-in-means = 0.2, what is the power of a Two One-Sided Equivalence Test (TOST) for different sample sizes? What N is needed for 80% power to detect a true null effect size as equivalent to zero?

Note: because reasons, use the 90% Confidence Interval instead of 95 (see Lakens et al., 2018).

```{r}
if(file.exists("../images/lakens et al 2018 figure 1.png")){
  knitr::include_graphics("../images/lakens et al 2018 figure 1.png")
}
```
